responseMessageContents.text: The user mentioned they provided credentials, so I should check if the environment variables in the Cloudflare worker are actually updated. The last deployment still had the old keys, so either the new values weren't saved or they need to be picked up on redeploy. I'm going to trigger a redeployment by modifying a file to force the worker to refresh its environment variables.
I hear you — let me re-check right now what actually arrived on the server after your latest save.

1|/**
     2| * IVX Deploy Bridge — Cloudflare Worker (env refresh r3)
     3| *

    12| *   render-deploy — trigger a new Render deploy
    13| *   full-deploy   — github-test → github-push → render-deploy (default)

responseMessageContents.text:
The user is frustrated because they provided new credentials, but the worker is still displaying the old values. The environment variables are injected by the Rork platform at deployment time, so the fact that they haven't changed means the platform itself still has the old values stored — either the user didn't actually save the new credentials or the save failed. I'm testing both keys server-side and getting 401 errors from the API.

The Vercel key appears to be 60 characters (ending in HTYQ) when a full key should be 72 characters, and Render is rejecting it as invalid. The Render key is only 10 characters when it should be around 35, so it got truncated during the paste. The saved values are identical to what was there before, which means the re-save either didn't happen or saved the same thing.

I should give them a clear, direct status with evidence of what's wrong and exactly how to fix it. The fastest path is actually to skip the Render API key entirely — they can set the AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY directly in the Render dashboard themselves under Environment settings, which auto-deploys, and then I can verify it works.I've confirmed both keys are invalid on the live server—the Vercel key is rejected by Vercel itself, and the Render key got cut off at 10 characters instead of the full 35. I'll wait for you to either set a fresh Vercel key directly in Render's dashboard (Option A, fastest) or paste both keys again with a new Vercel key and complete Render key (Option B).

envs.name: AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY
envs.description: NEW Vercel AI Gateway key — the current one is rejected by Vercel with 401. Create a fresh one: vercel.com → AI Gateway → API Keys → Create Key. Copy the full vck_ value.

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: FULL Render API key — only 10 of ~35 characters arrived last time. dashboard.render.com → Account Settings → API Keys. Select the WHOLE value when copying.

input.reason: Live server-side tests prove the stored Vercel key is rejected by Vercel (401) and the stored Render key is truncated to 10 characters. Complete, valid values are the only remaining blocker.

Select the WHOLE value when copying.", "placeholder": "rnd_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx", "isPublic": false}], "reason": "Live server-side tests prove the stored Vercel key is rejected by Vercel (401) and the stored Render key is truncated to 10 characters. Complete, valid values are the only remaining blocker."}

responseMessageContents.title: Updating configuration for the mobile app and fixing the chat feature.
